curmudgeon_review: multiple (ISS-370, ISS-383, ISS-422, ISS-427, ISS-432, ISS-440)
description: DOCX parity expansion: Add missing Sections 5.14–5.16 (repository code analysis), KILLSHOT-GAIA section, and fix hardcoded Self-Contradicted count. ISS-422 (major): Add three missing sections to Part 5. ISS-440 (major): Add Gaia Astrometry kill-shot section to Part 4. ISS-427 (major): Replace hardcoded '11' with computed tally['Self-Contradicted']. ISS-370 (minor): Fix WIN-056 framing. ISS-383 (minor): Split WIN-057/058. ISS-432 (minor): Fix cross-reference error in SEC-6.2.

code_changes.description: Replace hardcoded '11' for Self-Contradicted count with computed tally value. The DOCX currently hardcodes 11 in multiple locations; the current count from wins.json is 13. All hardcoded instances should use tally['Self-Contradicted'] for automatic accuracy.
code_changes.location: Line 191 (after tally summary text), Line 763 (Directive 1 analysis), Line 791 (Pattern 11), Line 812 (Final tally conclusion)

code_changes.description: Add Sections 5.14–5.16 to Part 5 of the DOCX (after Section 5.13, 'Summary: The AI Directives as a System'). These sections are present in the HTML but entirely missing from the DOCX build-doc-v4.js.
code_changes.location: After line 771-772 (after 'Summary: The AI Directives as a System' paragraph; before Part 6 title)

code_changes.description: Add KILLSHOT-GAIA section to Part 4 after the current '4.2 Antarctic Circumnavigation' and before '4.3 GPS Accuracy and Relativity' sections. The HTML has a complete Gaia Astrometry kill-shot section that is entirely absent from the DOCX.
code_changes.location: After Section 4.2 (Antarctic Circumnavigation), before Section 4.3 (GPS). Around line 1070-1075 in current build-doc-v4.js where Part 4 kill-shots are inserted.

code_changes.description: Fix WIN-056 framing to match HTML focus. Currently DOCX line 495 emphasizes H(r) circular fitting. HTML detail focuses on globe formula (23.45° axial tilt) substitution. Update to match the stronger argument.
code_changes.location: Around line 495-500 (WIN-056 detail section in DOCX)

code_changes.description: Split WIN-057 and WIN-058 into separate entries in the DOCX summary table. Currently they are combined into a single row despite having different verdicts (WIN-057: Misleading, WIN-058: Not Demonstrated). Each should have its own table row and separate detail section.
code_changes.location: DOCX Part 2.2 summary table, around line 475-500 (detail sections for WINs 057-058)

code_changes.description: Fix cross-reference error in Section 6.2 (Conclusions — Eclipse analysis). DOCX currently references 'Part 3.1' when it should reference 'Section 3.2' (the eclipse kill-shot section).
code_changes.location: Around line 1200+ (SEC-6.2 in DOCX, Conclusions section that references eclipse)

holes_addressed: ISS-370: WIN-056 framing (globe axial tilt vs. H(r) circularity) — addressed by reframing to emphasize globe parameter substitution.
ISS-383: WIN-057/058 conflation — addressed by providing separate table rows and detail sections with distinct verdicts (Misleading vs. Not Demonstrated).

ISS-422 (MAJOR): DOCX missing Sections 5.14–5.16 — addressed by adding ~1,200 words of code changes to build-doc-v4.js implementing all three sections with substantive content on inject_ai_layer.py, parameter drift, monitor.py adaptive tolerances, and eclipse escape clause.

ISS-427 (MAJOR): Self-Contradicted hardcoded count (11) — addressed by replacing all four instances (lines 191, 763, 791, 812) with tally['Self-Contradicted'] template literals, plus WIN-012 and WIN-053 added to the breakdown list.
ISS-432 (MINOR): SEC-6.2 cross-reference error (Part 3.1 → Section 3.2) — addressed by correcting reference.

ISS-440 (MAJOR): DOCX missing KILLSHOT-GAIA section — addressed by adding ~1,800 words of Gaia parallax analysis including distance-dependence test, differential parallax argument, New Horizons confirmation, chromatic dispersion refutation, and refraction objection rebuttal.
notes: EXP-011 is a comprehensive DOCX parity expansion targeting the major gap between HTML and DOCX content. The expansion includes three major additions (Sections 5.14–5.16, KILLSHOT-GAIA, hardcoded count fixes) and three minor fixes (WIN-056 framing, WIN-057/058 split, cross-reference). The code_changes array documents JavaScript code ready to paste into build-doc-v4.js — each change uses the existing helper functions (h1, h2, h3, p, pMixed, b, link) and matches the codebase's established style. All Gaia and Section 5.14–5.16 content is extracted from the current HTML (docs/index.html) and converted to docx-js format, preserving substantive arguments while stripping HTML markup. The hardcoded count fixes use template literals with the `tally` object (already computed at line 73–74) for automatic accuracy. Upon human application, run `node build.js docx` to regenerate the DOCX with all new content included. The DOCX will now have parity with the HTML on all major sections, kill-shot arguments, and computed values.
